name: get_drive_file
description: Read a Google Drive file by its id: returns the file metadata (name, mimeType, size, modifiedTime, webViewLink) plus a best-effort text rendering of its content. Google-native docs/sheets/slides are exported to text; binary files return text only if they decode as UTF-8, otherwise a note explains the content was omitted.

file_id.description: The Drive file id (from search_drive_files or a Drive URL).

operator.description: Optional. If omitted, resolves to the BlackBox's current operator (single operator auto-selected; otherwise the system default). Do not hard-code an operator name.

returns: JSON {id, name, mimeType, size, modifiedTime, webViewLink, content?} — `content` is included when a UTF-8 text rendering is feasible; otherwise a `note` field explains why (or an error string).
notes: Metadata is always returned. For a Google Doc/Slides you get plain text, for a Google Sheet you get CSV. A binary file (e.g. an image or zip) returns metadata + a `note` instead of `content`. Use search_drive_files first to find the file_id.
